pi_items.source: Task description explicitly states this path. setup_task.sh removes any pre-existing file at this path.
setup_task.sh Ruby seed script creates the project with identifier 'west-side-hwy'.
Task description explicitly requires these five columns.
Task description requires Priority to be 'High OR Urgent'. setup_task.sh creates these priorities.
Task description states 'not Closed'. Redmine's default closed statuses are Closed and Rejected.
setup_task.sh Ruby script creates exactly 5 matching target issues (3 High/New, 2 Urgent/New) and 8 distractors.

privileged_info_summary: The data is completely synthetic and generated during setup. A Ruby script initializes a Redmine project named 'west-side-hwy'. It creates exactly 13 issues: 5 target issues (3 High, 2 Urgent; all 'New' status) and 8 distractor issues (either 'Closed' status or 'Normal' priority). Therefore, the final correct report must contain exactly 5 rows and include the columns: Subject, Status, Priority, Due date, and Assignee.
